arguments.task: Open the GitHub REST API docs and then navigate as needed across official GitHub first-party documentation pages only. Read the strongest canonical pages supporting these fields for GitHub as an app/toolkit candidate: one-line description, documented authentication methods for the API, access/pricing or gating for using the API, API surface types (REST and/or GraphQL), API breadth, whether an official or existing MCP server exists, buildability today, rate limit notes, and notable signals. Do not rely on search-result snippets. Return strict JSON with keys: description, auth_methods_evidence [{method, notes, url}], access_tier_evidence {tier, notes, url}, api_surface {type, breadth, rest_url, graphql_url, mcp_exists, mcp_notes, mcp_url}, buildability {verdict, blocker, notes, url}, rate_limits {notes, url}, notable_signals [{text, url}], evidence_urls [] . Only cite live canonical official GitHub URLs you actually read.

input.thought: Start browser extraction from official GitHub docs to gather canonical evidence for all required fields.

raw_result.description: GitHub provides REST and GraphQL APIs to automate workflows, integrate with GitHub, and extend access to GitHub data and features.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs describe OAuth app authorization flows and token-based authentication using personal access tokens and GitHub App installation or user access tokens. The gathered evidence did not clearly support HTTP Basic or API key auth on current first-party API auth pages.
raw_result.gating_reason: GitHub documents Free, Pro, Team, and Enterprise plans, and the gathered evidence indicates API access is generally available without partner or admin approval; paid tiers mainly affect limits and some feature availability.

api_surface.breadth: Very broad public API surface across repositories, issues, pull requests, actions, apps, organizations, teams, projects, billing, security, and more via both REST and GraphQL.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Evidence gathered points to an existing GitHub MCP server in the official modelcontextprotocol/servers repository on GitHub.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Gathered evidence indicates REST rate limits of 60 requests/hour unauthenticated and 5,000 requests/hour authenticated, with higher limits up to 15,000/hour for some Enterprise Cloud contexts; GraphQL uses a point-based hourly limit and secondary limits also apply.
raw_result.notable_signals: Mature dual-surface API (REST and GraphQL), extensive official documentation, published rate-limit guidance, and an existing GitHub MCP server are strong signals for agent toolkit readiness.

result.description: GitHub provides REST and GraphQL APIs to automate workflows, integrate with GitHub, and extend access to GitHub data and features.
result.auth_notes: Official docs describe OAuth app authorization flows and token-based authentication using personal access tokens and GitHub App installation or user access tokens. The gathered evidence did not clearly support HTTP Basic or API key auth on current first-party API auth pages.
result.gating_reason: GitHub documents Free, Pro, Team, and Enterprise plans, and the gathered evidence indicates API access is generally available without partner or admin approval; paid tiers mainly affect limits and some feature availability.

api_surface.breadth: Very broad public API surface across repositories, issues, pull requests, actions, apps, organizations, teams, projects, billing, security, and more via both REST and GraphQL.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Evidence gathered points to an existing GitHub MCP server in the official modelcontextprotocol/servers repository on GitHub.

result.rate_limit_notes: Gathered evidence indicates REST rate limits of 60 requests/hour unauthenticated and 5,000 requests/hour authenticated, with higher limits up to 15,000/hour for some Enterprise Cloud contexts; GraphQL uses a point-based hourly limit and secondary limits also apply.
result.notable_signals: Mature dual-surface API (REST and GraphQL), extensive official documentation, published rate-limit guidance, and an existing GitHub MCP server are strong signals for agent toolkit readiness.
